Package edu.bu.pas.risk.agent

    • getAction

      public final Action getAction(GameView game, int actionCounter, boolean canRedeemCards)
      Returns this agent's choice of an action at the given game state.

      The Risk code will call this method many times, until you return a FortifyAction or a NoAction to end your turn. Every time this method is called, we increment the actionCounter by 1. This means at the start of your turn, it will always be 0.

      The order of actions is as follows:

      1. Any number, including 0 RedeemCardsActions
      2. Any number, including 0 AttackActions
      3. If eliminating an agent gives you 6 or more territory cards, then you must immediately trade in enough sets to reduce your hand to 4 or fewer cards. But once your hand is reduced to 4, 3, or 2 cards, you must stop trading.
      4. Exactly 1 of FortifyAction or NoAction (to skip fortify)
      Specified by:
      getAction in interface IAgent
      Parameters:
      game - The game state.
      actionCounter - How many actions you have taken so far this turn. Starts at 0 always.
      canRedeemCards - If you are allowed to send in a RedeemCardsAction. This is true when you have not started your attack phase, and have enough cards to trade in. This is also true when you have eliminated an opponent and now have 6 or more territory cards.
      Returns:
      The action you choose to do at this point in the game.

    • placeOneArmy

      public Territory placeOneArmy(GameView game, boolean isDuringSetup, int remainingArmies)
      During initial board setup, agents get to choose which territory they want. In this method, agents should choose an unclaimed territory (see TerritoryOwnerView.isUnclaimed()) to claim.

      During the setup phase, this method will be called MANY times, until the entire board is claimed. At each call, you are responsible for returning exactly 1 territory.

      The default implementation of this method randomly selects any unclaimed territory. 

      
         List<Territory> unownedTerritories = game.getUnownedTerritories();
         if (!unownedTerritories.isEmpty()) {
             // during the setup phase, we should always try to claim an
             // unclaimed territory. Consider biasing this random to claim
             // "chunks" of territories, or target specific continents.
             int randomIdx = game.getRandom().nextInt(unownedTerritories.size());
             return unownedTerritories.get(randomIdx);
         }

         // otherwise, just pick a territory we own at random
         List<Territory> ownedTerritories = game.getTerritoriesOwnedBy(this.agentId());
         int randomIdx = game.getRandom().nextInt(ownedTerritories.size());
         return ownedTerritories.get(randomIdx);
      Specified by:
      placeOneArmy in interface IAgent
      Parameters:
      game - The current game state.
      isDuringSetup - true if this is at the START of the game.
      remainingArmies - How many armies you have left to place.
      Returns:
      The territory you want to claim.
      See Also:
      • GameView.getUnownedTerritories()

      Parameters:

    • onTurnStart

      public void onTurnStart(GameView game, int agentIdx)
      Description copied from interface: RiskListener
      Called at the start of each agent's turn.
      Specified by:
      onTurnStart in interface RiskListener
      Parameters:
      game - The game state BEFORE an agent's turn starts.
      agentIdx - Whose turn it is.

    • onActionApplied

      public void onActionApplied(GameView game, int agentIdx, Action action, boolean success)
      Description copied from interface: RiskListener
      Called after any action is applied (attack, fortify, redeem, no-op).
      Specified by:
      onActionApplied in interface RiskListener
      Parameters:
      game - The game state AFTER the action has been applied.
      agentIdx - The idx of the agent who did the action.
      action - The actual action they took.
      success - Whether any special event occurred from the action. For example, in a AttackAction, this will be true when the attack fully CLAIMS the enemy territory, but otherwise returns false.

    • onTurnEnd

      public void onTurnEnd(GameView game, int agentIdx)
      Description copied from interface: RiskListener
      Called when a turn ends.
      Specified by:
      onTurnEnd in interface RiskListener
      Parameters:
      game - The game state AFTER the turn has ended and all actions have resolved.
      agentIdx - Whose turn it was.

      Parameters:
